definition.en: Your physical senses are extensions of these inner methods of perception, and after death it is upon these that you will rely.
definition.zh: 你们的肉体感官是这些内在感知方法的延伸，死后你们将依赖这些内在感官。

definition.en: Your physical senses are lovely liars that betray you constantly. They form reality for you, and yet the reality that they form is highly distorted; and what you see, dear friends, does not exist and you do not see what does exist.
definition.zh: 你们的肉体感官是可爱的骗子，不断背叛你们。它们为你们形成实相，然而它们形成的实相是高度扭曲的；你们所见的并不存在，而存在的你们却看不见。

definition.en: Your physical senses are therefore adapted to deal with a particular camouflage system. They are therefore only equipped to perceive the realities within the physical camouflage field.
definition.zh: 因此，你们的肉体感官被调整来应对一个特定的伪装系统。因此，它们只被配备去感知物质伪装场域内的实相。

definition.en: The physical senses are the extensions of inner senses that are, in one way or another, a part of each physical species regardless of its degree.
definition.zh: 肉体感官是内在感官的延伸，这些内在感官以某种方式属于每个物质物种，无论其程度如何。

definition.en: The physical senses are not, underline not, utilized. At the moment of perception the traveling consciousness perceives through the inner senses, and by itself automatically makes the necessary adjustments so that the ego can perceive the data in its accustomed way.
definition.zh: 肉体感官并未被利用，强调并未被利用。在感知的瞬间，旅行的意识通过内在感官感知，并自动做出必要的调整，以便自我能够以习惯的方式感知数据。

explanation: 物理感官是自我在物质系统内感知现实的工具，但它们并非感知的唯一途径。物理感官将无限、同时存在的现实转化为线性的、片段化的物理数据，因此它们本质上扭曲了现实。物理感官不仅被动感知，还主动参与创造它们所呈现的物质世界——它们是“伪装”的一部分，像透镜一样迫使你将能量场解读为物质形式。因此，物理感官只能告诉你表面现象，无法揭示时间、空间和物质背后的真实本质。要触及更深层的现实，需要暂时关闭物理感官，依赖内在感官或梦境状态，这些方式能让你直接体验灵魂的感知方式。物理感官的运作也受信念影响：你相信什么，感官就会强化并实现什么。最终，物理感官是自我在物质舞台上的工具，但真正的现实远超它们所能捕捉的范围。

quotes.text: It goes without saying then that the soul does not require a physical body for purposes of perception; that perception is not dependent upon physical senses; that experience continues whether or not you are in this life or another; and also that the soul's basic methods of perception are also operating within you now even as you read this book. It also follows that your experience within the physical system is dependent upon a physical form and physical senses -- again, because these interpret reality and translate it into physical data. It also follows that some hints of the soul's direct experience can be gained by momentarily switching the physical senses off -- by refusing to use them as perceptors, and falling back upon other methods. Now you do this to some extent in the dream state, but even then in many dreams you still tend to translate experience into hallucinatory physical terms. Most of the dreams that you recall are of this nature.
quotes.translation: 不言而喻，灵魂并不需要肉体来进行感知；感知并不依赖于物质感官；无论你处于此生还是他世，体验都会持续；并且，即使在你阅读本书的此刻，灵魂的基本感知方式也正在你之内运作。由此可知，你在物质系统内的体验确实依赖于物质形态和物质感官——再次强调，因为它们将实相诠释并转化为物质数据。同样可知，通过暂时关闭物质感官——拒绝将其作为感知工具，转而依靠其他方法——可以获得灵魂直接体验的些许线索。你在梦境状态中某种程度上做到了这一点，但即便在梦中，你仍倾向于将体验转化为幻觉性的物质术语。你所回忆的大多数梦境都属于这种性质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You must perceive what you do of reality through your physical senses, but your physical senses distort reality. They present reality to you in their own way. The physical senses can only perceive reality a little bit at a time, and so it seems to you that one moment exists, and is gone forever, and the next moment comes, and like the one before it disappears. But everything in the universe exists at one time, simultaneously, and the first words ever spoken still ring throughout the universe; and in your terms the last words ever spoken have been said time and time again, for there is no ending and no beginning. It is only your perception that is limited.
quotes.translation: 你必须通过物质感官来感知你所体验的实相，但物质感官扭曲了实相。它们以自己的方式向你呈现实相。物质感官一次只能感知实相的一小部分，因此对你而言，一个瞬间存在，然后永远消失，下一个瞬间到来，又像前一个一样消失。但宇宙中的一切同时存在于一个时间点，最初说出的话语至今仍在宇宙中回响；而用你的话说，最终说出的话语已被反复言说无数次，因为没有终点也没有起点。只是你的感知受到了限制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (And once again, as she'd done for the session of last December 28, she wanted tonight's material to be presented as part of regular Session 393. I was glad to do it--and note that Jane's class sessions usually are in marked contrast to those we hold for ourselves, for books or whatever in the future. As would be expected of a gathering, class sessions were full of energy and repartee and questions and serious thinking both with and without Seth.

quotes.text: Within him there are methods of perception that allow him to see through the camouflage settings, to see beyond the stage. He uses these inner senses constantly, though the actor part of himself is so intent upon the play that this escapes him. In a large manner, the physical senses actually form the physical reality they seem to only perceive. They are themselves part of the camouflage, but they are like lenses over your natural inner perceptions that force you to "see" an available field of activity as physical matter; and so they can be relied upon only to tell you what is happening in a superficial manner. You can tell the position of the other actors for example, or time by clock, but these physical senses will not tell you that time is itself a camouflage, or that consciousness forms the other actors, or that realities that you cannot see exist over and beyond the physical matter that is so apparent.
quotes.translation: 在他之内存在着感知方法，使他能够看穿伪装设定，看到舞台之外。他不断运用这些内在感官，尽管他自身作为演员的那部分如此专注于戏剧，以至于这被他忽略了。从很大程度上说，物质感官实际上形成了它们似乎仅仅感知到的物质实相。它们本身就是伪装的一部分，但它们就像覆盖在你自然内在感知上的透镜，迫使你"看见"一个可用的活动场域作为物质；因此，只能依赖它们以肤浅的方式告诉你正在发生什么。例如，你可以知道其他演员的位置，或时钟上的时间，但这些物质感官不会告诉你时间本身就是一种伪装，或者意识形成了其他演员，或者你无法看见的实相存在于如此明显的物质之上和之外。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It may outrage your intellect, and the evidence of your physical senses may shout that it is untrue, yet a belief in good without a belief in evil is actually highly realistic, since in physical life it will keep your body healthier, keep you psychologically free of many fears and mental difficulties, and bring you a feeling of ease and spontaneity in which the development of your abilities can be better fulfilled. After death it will release you from the belief in demons and hell, and enforced punishment. You will be better prepared to understand the nature of reality as it is. I understand that the concept does indeed offend your intellect, and that your senses seem to deny it. Yet you should already realize that your senses tell you many things, which are not true; and I tell you that your physical senses perceive a reality that is a result of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 这可能冒犯你的理智，你的物质感官的证据可能大声宣称这是不真实的，然而相信善而不相信恶实际上是非常现实的，因为在物质生活中，它会让你的身体更健康，让你在心理上摆脱许多恐惧和精神困扰，并带给你一种轻松和自发的感觉，在这种感觉中，你的能力发展可以得到更好的实现。死后，它将使你摆脱对恶魔和地狱的信仰，以及强制惩罚。你将更好地准备理解实相的本质。我理解这个概念确实冒犯了你的理智，你的感官似乎否定了它。然而你应该已经意识到，你的感官告诉你的许多事情并不真实；我告诉你，你的物质感官感知到的实相是你信念的结果。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now at times I will be using the term "camouflage," referring to the physical world to which the outer ego relates, for physical form is one of the camouflages that reality adopts. The camouflage is real, and yet there is a much greater reality within it -- the vitality that gave it form. Your physical senses then allow you to perceive this camouflage, for they are attuned to it in a highly specialized manner. But to sense the reality within the form requires a different sort of attention, and more delicate manipulations than the physical senses provide.
quotes.translation: 现在，有时我会使用"伪装"这个术语，指的是外在自我所关联的物质世界，因为物质形态是实相所采用的伪装之一。伪装是真实的，但其中存在着更大的实相——赋予它形态的生命力。你的物质感官因此允许你感知这种伪装，因为它们以高度专业化的方式与之调谐。但要感知形态内的实相，需要一种不同于物质感官所提供的关注和更精细的操作。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: A psychological experience may take up no space as a rock takes up space, but when a psychological experience happens it may fill you up. Yet you do not deny the existence of a psychological experience, though you cannot rip it apart from yourself and examine it with the physical senses. Still it has its effect, and its validity is well known to every man. So also are there other realities that cannot be examined through the use of the physical senses, realities so close to the self that they cannot be separated from it and objectified.
quotes.translation: 心理体验可能不像岩石占据空间那样占据空间，但当心理体验发生时，它可能充满你。然而你并不否认心理体验的存在，尽管你无法将其从自身剥离并用物质感官检查。但它仍然有其效果，其有效性对每个人来说都是众所周知的。同样，还有其他实相无法通过物质感官来检查，这些实相如此贴近自我，以至于无法与之分离并客观化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now it is nearly impossible to separate a discussion of the nature of the soul from a discussion of the nature of perception. Very briefly let us review a few points: You form physical matter and the physical world that you know. The physical senses actually can be said to create the physical world, in that they force you to perceive an available field of energy in physical terms, and impose a highly specialized pattern upon this field of reality. Using the physical senses, you can perceive reality in no other way.
quotes.translation: 现在几乎不可能将灵魂本质的讨论与感知本质的讨论分开。让我们简要回顾几点：你形成了你所知的物质实相与物质世界。物理感官实际上可以说创造了物质世界，因为它们迫使你以物质术语感知可用的能量场，并将高度专门化的模式强加于这个实相场。使用物理感官时，你无法以其他方式感知实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The same kind of development can occur in almost any physical category. Usually more than one belief is involved. Parallel with the belief that vision will fail, you may have the before-mentioned belief that hearing will dim, and these two ideas may be reinforced by a belief that age automatically makes you less a person, turning you into an individual who can no longer relate in the daily pattern of environment. The belief, you see, would work to insure the materialization of that state. (Pause.) On the other hand you may believe that wisdom grows with age, that self-understanding brings a peace of mind not earlier known, that the keen mind is actually far better able to assess the environment, and that the physical senses are much more appreciative of all stimuli. And so those conditions will be physically met in your experience. The physical apparatus itself, following your beliefs, will continue in health.
quotes.translation: 几乎任何物质范畴都可能发生类似的发展。通常涉及不止一种信念。与视力将衰退的信念并行，你可能持有前述的听力将变弱的信念，而这两种想法可能被"年龄自动使你不再完整，将你变成无法再参与日常环境模式的个体"这一信念所强化。你看，这个信念会确保那种状态的物质化。（停顿。）另一方面，你可能相信智慧随年龄增长，自我理解带来前所未有的心灵平静，敏锐的心智实际上更能评估环境，物理感官对所有刺激的感知更为敏锐。于是这些条件将在你的体验中物质性地实现。遵循你的信念，物理装置本身将持续保持健康。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When you are born, you understand that you have a new consciousness. You explore its ramifications. It is your primary evidence that you exist in flesh. Basically, each person must confront the experience of reality through a direct encounter with it. This encounter takes place through the use of the physical senses, of course, as they are used to perceive and interpret physical data. The very utilization of those senses, however, is dependent upon the nature of your consciousness itself, and that consciousness is aware of its power and action through the exercise of its own properties.
quotes.translation: 当你出生时，你明白自己拥有新的意识。你探索其分支。这是你存在于肉身的主要证据。从根本上说，每个人都必须通过直接面对实相来体验它。这种面对当然通过使用物理感官发生，因为它们被用来感知和解读物质资料。然而，这些感官的运用本身取决于你意识本质的特性，而该意识通过行使自身属性来认知其力量与行动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am not comparing personality to an orange or an onion, but I want to emphasize that as these things grow from within outward, so does each fragment of the entire self. You observe the outside aspect of objects. Your physical senses permit you to perceive the exterior forms to which you then react, but your physical senses to some extent force you to perceive reality in this manner, and the inside vitality within matter and form is not so apparent.
quotes.translation: 我并非将人格比作橙子或洋葱，但我想强调：正如这些事物由内向外生长，整个自性的每个片段也是如此。你观察物体的外在面向。你的物理感官允许你感知外在形态并对其做出反应，但物理感官在某种程度上迫使你以这种方式感知实相，而物质与形态内在的生命力并不明显。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As you create physical matter constantly without knowing that you do so, so also you create constantly a dream universe, and this dream universe is as individual as your environment in the physical world. There is also a chemical reaction here, for without dreaming the physical organism could not exist. With your outer senses, you perceive but the camouflage physical reality which the physical senses are equipped to perceive. In the dream universe you are however free, and familiar, with both space and time in a manner which is denied you in the waking state. Where indeed are your dream locations? Where in space is the street upon which you walk in a dream?
quotes.translation: 正如你在不知不觉中持续创造物质实相，你也持续创造梦境宇宙，这个梦境宇宙与你在物质世界的环境一样独特。这里也存在化学反应，因为没有梦境，物质有机体就无法存在。借助外在感官，你只能感知物理感官被设计去感知的伪装物质实相。然而在梦境宇宙中，你既自由又熟悉时空，其方式在清醒状态下是被禁止的。你梦境中的地点究竟在哪里？你梦中行走的街道在空间中的何处？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: (All with emphatic rhythm:) The inner self was too aware of its own multidimensionality, so in your terms it gave psychological birth to itself through the body in space and time. It knew itself as a physical creature. That portion of the self is the portion you recognize as your usual conscious self, alive within the scheme of seasons, aware within the designs of time, caught transfixed in moments of brilliant awareness, with civilizations that seem to come and go. That is the self that is alert in the dear preciseness of the moments, whose physical senses are bound to light and darkness, sound and touch. That is the self that lives the life of the body.
quotes.translation: （全部以强调的节奏：）内在自性过于觉察自身的多维性，因此用你们的话说，它通过身体在时空中的存在而给予自己心理上的诞生。它将自己认知为物质生物。自性的这一部分就是你认作平常意识自我的部分，在季节更替中活跃，在时间设计中觉察，在璀璨觉知的瞬间凝固，目睹文明似乎来去匆匆。这就是在珍贵精确的瞬间保持警觉的自我，其物理感官被束缚于光明与黑暗、声音与触觉。这就是活出身体生命的自我。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner senses are equipped to perceive data that is not physical. They are not deceived by the images that you project in three-dimensional reality. Now, they can perceive physical objects. Your physical senses are extensions of these inner methods of perception, and after death it is upon these that you will rely. They are used in out-of-body experiences. They operate constantly beneath normal waking consciousness...
quotes.translation: 内在感官能够感知非物质的资料。它们不会被你在三维实相中投射的影像所欺骗。它们确实能感知物质对象。你的肉体感官是这些内在感知方式的延伸，死后你将依赖它们。它们被用于出体经验中，在正常的清醒意识之下持续运作……
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In dreams, when you expect none, none appear. Out of body experiences indeed can be a health boon. Your system is automatically relaxed and free of pressure. Also, incidentally, free of time. It does not age during a projection; that is, the body does not age. It is in suspension. Any interpretations that are made are made by consciousness itself. The physical senses are not， underline not, utilized. At the moment of perception the traveling consciousness perceives through the inner senses, and by itself automatically makes the necessary adjustments so that the ego can perceive the data in its accustomed way.
quotes.translation: 在梦境中，当你预期没有影像时，它们便不会出现。出体经验确实能带来健康益处。你的系统会自动放松，没有压力。顺便一提，也没有时间。在投射期间它不会老化；也就是说，身体不会老化。它处于悬浮状态。任何诠释都由意识本身进行。肉体感官并未被利用——注意，是未被利用。在感知的瞬间，旅行中的意识通过内在感官进行感知，并自动做出必要调整，以便自我能以惯常方式接收资料。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: This does not, however, mean that this is the only reality. It is simply the only reality that you perceive with the physical senses. In order to perceive other realities, you must therefore switch from your outer senses to the inner senses, for the inner senses are clearer, and are equipped to perceive action and reality as it exists independently of the distortions given to it by the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 但这并不意味着这是唯一的实相。它只是你用肉体感官所感知到的唯一实相。为了感知其他实相，你必须从外在感官切换到内在感官，因为内在感官更清晰，能够感知到独立于肉体感官扭曲之外的行动与实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The next chapter will deal with the basic methods of communication that are used by any consciousness, according to its degree, whether or not it is physical. This will lead up to the basic communication used by human personalities as you understand them, and point out these inner communications as existing independently of the physical senses, which are merely physical extensions of inner perception.
quotes.translation: 下一章将讨论任何意识根据其程度所使用的基本沟通方法，无论它是否具有物质形态。这将引导出你所理解的人类人格所使用的基本沟通方式，并指出这些内在沟通独立于肉体感官而存在，而肉体感官只是内在感知的物质延伸。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Here is a copy of that outline, which was given to us in Session 510, January 19, 1970. As you'll see here, Seth calls me Ruburt, and Rob, Joseph. These names represent our entire personalities as distinguished from our present physically oriented selves.

quotes.text: It is quite true that your physical senses create the reality that they perceive. A tree is something far different to a microbe, a bird, an insect, and a man who stands beneath it. I am not saying that the tree only appears to be different. It is different. You perceive its reality through one set of highly specialized senses. This does not mean that its reality exists in that form in any more basic way than it exists in the form perceived by the microbe, insect, or bird. You cannot perceive the quite valid reality of that tree in any context but your own. This applies to anything within the physical system that you know.
quotes.translation: 确实，你的肉体感官创造了它们所感知的实相。一棵树对微生物、鸟、昆虫和树下的人来说，是截然不同的东西。我并非说树只是看起来不同——它确实不同。你通过一套高度专门化的感官来感知它的实相。但这并不意味着它的实相以那种形式存在，就比微生物、昆虫或鸟类所感知的形式更为基本。除了你自己的脉络，你无法感知那棵树相当真实的实相。这适用于你所知的物质系统内的任何事物。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Anything that you can see and feel and touch exists within your own reality, yes, and within it, it is legitimate and valid. It is also highly distorted, for your physical senses are lovely liars that betray you constantly. They form reality for you, and yet the reality that they form is highly distorted; and what you see, dear friends, does not exist and you do not see what does exist.
quotes.translation: 任何你能看见、感觉和触摸到的东西，确实存在于你自己的实相中，并且在此实相中它是合理且有效的。但它也高度扭曲，因为你的肉体感官是可爱的说谎者，不断背叛你。它们为你构建实相，然而它们构建的实相高度扭曲；亲爱的朋友们，你们所看见的并不存在，而你们看不见真正存在的东西。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The first journey from one home station to another, unfamiliar one may bring you in contact with various kinds of bleed-throughs, distortions, or static. These can be expected. They are simply the result of not yet learning how to tune your own consciousness clearly in to other kinds of focus. Before you can pick up the "next" station, for example, you may see ghost images in your mind, or pick up distorted versions from your own home station. You have momentarily dispensed with the usual, habitual organizational process by which you unite regular physical sense perceptions, so while you are "between stations," you may well encounter mixed signals from each. When you alter your conscious focus in such a fashion, you are also moving away from the part of your psyche that you consider its center. You are journeying through your own psyche, in other words, for different realities are different states of the psyche -- materialized, projected outward and experienced. That applies to your home station or physical world as well.
quotes.translation: 从一个本然站台前往另一个陌生站台的首次旅程，可能会让你接触到各种类型的渗透、扭曲或静电干扰。这些现象是可以预期的。它们只是你尚未学会如何将自己的意识清晰地调频到其他焦点类型的结果。例如，在你能够接收到"下一个"站台之前，你可能会在脑海中看到鬼影般的影像，或从自己的本然站台接收到扭曲的版本。你暂时放下了通常用来整合常规物理感官知觉的习惯性组织过程，因此当你处于"站台之间"时，你很可能会遇到来自各个站台的混合信号。当你以这种方式改变意识焦点时，你也在远离你视为自己心理核心的那部分。换句话说，你正在穿越自己的心理，因为不同的实相就是心理的不同状态——物质化、向外投射并被体验。这也适用于你的本然站台或物质世界。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: At such times I'm apt to think about ideas of reincarnation and counterparts. Right here I'm dealing with just two of Seth's larger concepts. But without dwelling upon them too heavily, I may consider the notion of my larger, nonphysical "whole self" or "entity" being made up of a number of other psychically related physical selves projected into time. For Seth, basically there is no time, only a great "spacious present" that's a manifestation of a sublime, indescribable All That Is. Our gross physical senses, and indeed our very bodies, insist upon interpreting the spacious present in linear terms, however--through the inevitable processes of birth, aging, and death--so to help us get his point here Seth advances his ideas of reincarnational selves and counterpart selves in ways we can understand sensually.
quotes.translation: 在这种时候，我倾向于思考转世和对应体的概念。此刻我处理的只是赛斯两个更宏大的概念。但不过分深究它们的话，我可以考虑这样一个概念：我那个更宏大的、非物质的"整体自己"或"存有"是由许多其他心理上相关的、投射到时间中的物质自己组成的。对赛斯来说，基本上没有时间，只有一个伟大的"广阔当下"，它是那个崇高、难以描述的"一切万有"的显现。然而，我们粗重的物理感官，乃至我们的身体本身，都坚持用线性方式来诠释这个广阔当下——通过出生、衰老和死亡这些不可避免的过程——所以为了帮助我们理解他的观点，赛斯在此以我们能感官理解的方式提出了他的转世自己和对应体自己的概念。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Even in God of Jane, which was published in 1981, Jane presented some relatively late material from Seth to show that he doesn't independently communicate with others. The idea that he'd done so can be inspiring, however. In Chapter 20 of her book, see Session 876 for August 27, 1979. After explaining how a couple of women (among others) had recently claimed that he had been in contact with them, Seth stated: "Now, I did not communicate with those women--but their belief in me helped each of them use certain abilities."
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: As for others all fragments have （pause） are throw offs or projections. Difficult to explain, I am not doing well. In a physical sense this board is a projection of wood or a tree, but in this case the board has less properties than the parent tree. The tree can grow, the board cannot. A personality fragment on the other hand never has less properties than its parent. This is the difference. A personality fragment has all the properties of its parents inherent, though it may not know how to use them. The board however cannot learn to grow, even though you stick it in the earth.
quotes.translation: 至于其他，所有片段都具有（停顿）是抛出物或投射物。很难解释，我做得不好。在物理意义上，这块木板是木头或树的投射，但在这个例子中，木板比母树具有更少的属性。树可以生长，木板却不能。另一方面，人格片段从不比其母体具有更少的属性。这就是区别。人格片段具有其母体固有的所有属性，尽管它可能不知道如何使用它们。然而，即使你把木板插进土里，它也无法学会生长。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Consciousness is a way of perceiving the various dimensions of reality. Consciousness as you know it is highly specialized. The physical senses allow you to perceive the three-dimensional world, and yet by their very nature they can inhibit the perception of other equally valid dimensions. Most of you identify with your daily physically oriented self. You would not think of identifying with one portion of your body and ignoring all other parts, and yet you are doing the same thing (smile) when you imagine that the egotistical self carries the burden of your identity.
quotes.translation: 意识是一种感知实相各个维度的方式。你所知道的意识是高度专业化的。物理感官让你能够感知三维世界，然而正是由于它们的本质，它们会抑制对其他同样有效维度的感知。你们大多数人认同于自己日常的、以物质为导向的自己。你不会想到认同于自己身体的一部分而忽略所有其他部分，然而当你想象自我性的自己承载着你身份的负担时，你就在做同样的事情（微笑）。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It understands, for example, that clairvoyant material is a part of the personality's overall characteristics, so it is not afraid of perceiving it -- and it is able to separate such information confusion from present physical sense perception. Orderliness, then, is one of its main characteristics. When it is given only one world view, and only one group of assumptions, its orderly nature causes it to throw out all information that does not fit. It is almost forced to make an orderly picture, say like a jigsaw puzzle picture, while being denied half of the pieces.
quotes.translation: 例如，它理解千里眼材料是人格整体特征的一部分，所以它不害怕感知它——并且它能够将这种信息混淆与当前的物理感官知觉区分开来。因此，有序性是它的主要特征之一。当它只被给予一种世界观和一组假设时，它有序的本性会导致它丢弃所有不符合的信息。它几乎被迫要拼出一幅有序的图画，就像拼图游戏一样，却同时被剥夺了一半的拼块。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: In the beginning, man's dreams were in certain terms of immediate physical survival. They gave man information--a kind that of necessity the new physical senses could not contain. Those senses could only perceive the immediate environment, but man's dreams compensated for that lack, and filled out his consciousness by giving it the benefit of that larger generalized information to which it had once had an easy access. When he was asleep man could take advantage of the information banks contained in the units of consciousness that composed his very flesh.
quotes.translation: 起初，人类的梦境在某种程度上直接关乎物理生存。它们给人类提供信息——一种新的物理感官必然无法包含的信息。那些感官只能感知直接环境，但人类的梦境弥补了这一缺陷，并通过赋予它曾经易于获取的那些更广泛的概括性信息的好处，来充实它的意识。当人类睡着时，他可以利用构成他血肉的意识单位中所包含的信息库。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some portion of each individual is in direct contact with the very source of its own existence. Each individual is innately aware that help is available in every situation, and that information does not need to come through the physical senses alone. Many illnesses are cured, then, through quite natural methods that not only involve physical healings, but bring into play other events -- events that have great bearing on the psychological elements that may be involved behind the scenes. For those interactions we will have to look to Framework 2.
quotes.translation: 每个个体的某一部分都与自身存在的源头直接相连。每个个体天生就知道，在任何情况下都能获得帮助，而且信息不必仅通过肉体感官来获取。因此，许多疾病是通过相当自然的方法治愈的，这些方法不仅涉及肉体疗愈，还会引发其他事件——这些事件对可能隐藏在幕后的心理因素有着重大影响。对于这些互动，我们必须求助于框架二。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The physical senses usually blind you to these open channels, for they perceive reality only in their own image. To some extent, then, I enter your reality through a psychological warp in your space and time. In a manner of speaking, such an open channel serves much as a pathway between Ruburt's personality and my own, so that communication is possible between. Such psychological and psychic warps between dimensions of existence are not infrequent. They are merely recognized as such infrequently and utilized even less so.
quotes.translation: 肉体感官通常会让你对这些开放通道视而不见，因为它们只按自己的形象感知实相。在某种程度上，我是通过你们时空中的一个心理扭曲进入你们的实相的。可以说，这样的开放通道就像鲁柏人格与我人格之间的一条路径，使得沟通成为可能。存在维度之间的这种心理和心灵扭曲并不罕见。只是它们很少被如此识别，更少被利用。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Our environment therefore is composed of exquisite imbalances, where change is allowed full play. Your own time structure misleads you into your ideas of the relative permanency of physical matter, and you close your eyes to the constant alterations within it. Your physical senses confine you as best they can to the perception of a highly formalized reality. Only through the use of the intuitions and in sleep and dream states, as a rule, can you perceive the joyfully changing nature of your own, and any, consciousness.
quotes.translation: 因此，我们的环境由精妙的不平衡构成，变化在其中得以充分展现。你们的时间结构误导你们产生对物质实相相对恒久性的观念，而你们对其中不断的变化视而不见。你们的肉体感官尽可能地将你们限制在对高度形式化实相的感知中。通常，只有通过运用直觉，以及在睡眠和梦境状态中，你们才能感知到自己以及任何意识的喜悦变化的本质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: The senses that you use, in a very real manner, create the environment that you perceive. Your physical senses necessitate the perception of a three-dimensional reality. Consciousness is equipped with inner perceptors, however. These are inherent within all consciousness, regardless of its development. These perceptors operate quite independently from those that might be assumed when a given consciousness adopts a specialized form, such as a physical body, in order to operate in a particular system.
quotes.translation: 现在：你们所使用的感官，在非常真实的意义上，创造了你们所感知的环境。你们的肉体感官必然导致对三维实相的感知。然而，意识配备了内在感知器。这些感知器内在于所有意识之中，无论其发展程度如何。当某个意识为了在特定系统中运作而采用专门形式（如肉体）时，这些感知器的运作完全独立于可能被假定的那些感知器。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: No. It is his tendency to gobble here that is important. He has given up in this life gobbling food and drink. In past lives he was never temperate, neither in a physical sense, emotionally or intellectually. This was not in many ways bad. However when the intemperate personality does not discriminate then the basic characteristic can cause unpleasantness. He was always very generous for example and kind, even overly kind. On the other hand he ate and drank too much. He was just overeager as a rule.
quotes.translation: 不。重要的是他在这里狼吞虎咽的倾向。在这一生中，他已经放弃了狼吞虎咽食物和饮料。在过去的生命中，他从未节制过，无论是在肉体感官上、情感上还是智力上。这在很多方面并非坏事。然而，当不节制的个性不加区分时，基本特征就会导致不愉快。例如，他总是非常慷慨和善良，甚至过于善良。另一方面，他吃喝过多。通常他只是过于急切。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I call this seemingly unconscious the "inner ego," for it directs inner activities. It correlates information that is perceived not through the physical senses, but through other inner channels. It is the inner perceiver of reality that exists beyond the three-dimensional. It carries within it the memory of each of your past existences. It looks into subjective dimensions that are literally infinite, and from these subjective dimensions all objective realities flow. (Long pause.)
quotes.translation: 我将这个看似无意识的部分称为“内在自我”，因为它指导内在活动。它关联的不是通过肉体感官感知的信息，而是通过其他内在渠道感知的信息。它是存在于三维之外实相的内在感知者。它内在携带着你每一世存在的记忆。它审视着字面上无限的主观维度，而所有客观实相都从这些主观维度中流出。（长停顿。）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is not that physical reality is false. It is that the physical picture is simply one of an infinite number of ways of perceiving the various guises through which consciousness expresses itself. The physical senses force you to translate experience into physical perceptions. The inner senses open your range of perception, allow you to interpret experience in a far freer manner and to create new forms and new channels through which you, or any consciousness, can know itself.
quotes.translation: 并非物质实相是虚假的。而是物质画面只是无数种感知方式之一，通过这些方式，意识得以展现其各种伪装。物质感官迫使你将经验转化为物质感知。内在感官则拓展你的感知范围，让你能以远更自由的方式诠释经验，并创造新的形式与渠道，使你自己或任何意识得以认识自身。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now these abilities operate whether you know it or not, but often they operate in spite of you rather than with your conscious cooperation; and often when you do find yourself using them, you become frightened, disoriented, or confused. No matter what you have been taught, you must understand, for example, that basically speaking, perceptions are not physical in the way the term is usually used. If you catch yourself perceiving information through other than your physical senses, then you must accept the fact that this is the way perception works.
quotes.translation: 无论你是否知晓，这些能力都在运作，但往往它们是在违背你意愿的情况下运作，而非得到你有意识的合作；而且当你发现自己正在运用它们时，常常会感到恐惧、迷失方向或困惑。无论你曾被教导什么，你必须明白，例如，基本上来说，感知并非通常意义上所说的物质性。如果你发现自己通过物质感官以外的途径接收信息，那么你必须接受这个事实：感知本就是如此运作的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I must explain this before I can clearly give you an idea of my environment, or of those other systems of reality in which I operate. There is no space between my environment and yours, for example, no physical boundaries that separate us. In a very real way of speaking, your concept of reality as seen through your physical senses, scientific instruments, or arrived at through deduction, bears little resemblance to the facts -- and the facts are difficult to explain.
quotes.translation: 我必须先解释这一点，才能清楚地让你了解我的环境，或我所运作的其他实相系统。例如，我的环境与你的环境之间并无空间间隔，也没有任何物质界限将我们分开。从非常真实的角度来说，你通过物质感官、科学仪器所看到的，或通过推理得出的实相概念，与事实几乎毫无相似之处——而事实本身是难以解释的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Every thought in one way or another is constructed by you in physical terms. You cannot escape the result of one thought. Every thought is an actuality. It affects every action and it forms your physical environment. As you sit listening to my voice, you are all subconsciously forming the physical environment of your classroom. You are forming the physical chairs. You are forming the blackboard. You are all forming reality, as you know it. Then with your physical senses, you are perceiving that which you have already so created. If you do not like what you see, then who is to blame? For these are your own constructions , formed in faithful replica to your own thoughts. We do not have the time to explain now how this is done, but in the material we have explained it. Certain telepathic connections exist which are what we term root assumptions, of which you are all telepathically aware. Using these you form a physical environment that is cohesive enough so that you can all agree with what you see and feel and smell and touch.
quotes.translation: 每一个念头，都以某种方式被你用物质形式构建出来。你无法逃脱任何一个念头的后果。每一个念头都是一个实相。它影响每一个行动，并塑造你的物质环境。当你坐着聆听我的声音时，你们都在潜意识中构建着教室的物质环境。你们在构建物质性的椅子。你们在构建黑板。你们都在构建你们所知的实相。然后，你们用物质感官去感知你们已经创造出来的东西。如果你不喜欢你所看到的，那该怪谁呢？因为这些都是你自己的构建，忠实地复制了你自己的思想。我们现在没有时间解释这是如何做到的，但在资料中我们已经解释过了。存在某些心灵感应的联系，我们称之为根本假设，你们所有人都能心灵感应地意识到它们。利用这些假设，你们构建出一个足够凝聚的物质环境，使你们都能对所看、所感、所闻、所触达成一致。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: In such cases however definite atoms and molecules are utilized. Now. In our seance we were, again, manipulating energy and forming objects from it--objects that you could perceive with your physical senses. The whole matter, if you will forgive my pun （smile） on matter is highly intriguing, and we shall have several sessions at least, dealing with the reality of sense data, and the unreality, comparatively speaking, of matter itself.
quotes.translation: 然而，在这些情况下，确实利用了明确的原子和分子。现在。在我们的降神会中，我们再次操控能量，并从中形成物体——你们能用物质感官感知到的物体。请原谅我关于“物质”的双关语（微笑），整个事情非常引人入胜，我们至少会进行几节课，探讨感官资料的真实性，以及相对而言，物质本身的不真实性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: There is, in no circumstance, any closed system. Action therefore flows within all systems and realities. Your physical senses are therefore adapted to deal with a particular camouflage system. They are therefore only equipped to perceive the realities within the physical camouflage field.
quotes.translation: 在任何情况下，都不存在封闭的系统。因此，行动在所有系统和实相中流动。你们的物质感官因此被设计来应对特定的伪装系统。它们因此只配备来感知物质伪装场域内的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The physical senses are the extensions of inner senses 3 that are, in one way or another, a part of each physical species regardless of its degree. The inner senses provide all species with an inner method of communication. The c-e-l-l-s (spelled out), then, possess inner senses.
quotes.translation: 肉体感官是内在感官3的延伸，无论程度如何，它们都以某种方式成为每个物质物种的一部分。内在感官为所有物种提供内在的沟通方式。细胞（逐字母拼出）因此也拥有内在感官。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: In a manner of speaking the activity of your brain adjusts the speed with which you, as a physical creature, perceive life's events. Theoretically, those events could be slowed down or run at a quicker pace. Again in a manner of speaking, the sound, vision, dimensional solidarity and so forth are "dubbed in." The picture runs at the same speed, more or less. The physical senses chime in together to give you a dramatic sensual chorus, each "voice" keeping perfect time with all the other sensual patterns so that as a rule there is harmony and a sense of continuity, with no embarrassing lapses.
quotes.translation: 可以这么说，你大脑的活动调节着你作为物质生物感知生命事件的速度。理论上，这些事件可以被放慢或加快。同样可以这么说，声音、视觉、三维立体感等是"后期配音"进去的。画面大致以相同速度运行。肉体感官共同协作，为你呈现一场戏剧性的感官合唱，每个"声音"与其他所有感官模式保持完美同步，因此通常会有和谐与连续感，没有令人尴尬的中断。

quotes.text: The experiment that would transform your world would operate upon the basic idea that you create your own reality according to the nature of your beliefs, and that all existence was blessed, and that evil did not exist in it. If these ideas were followed individually and collectively, then the evidence of your physical senses would find no contradiction. They would perceive the world and existence as good.
quotes.translation: 能够改变你世界的实验，应基于这样一个基本理念：你根据自己信念的性质创造自己的实相，所有存在都受到祝福，邪恶并不存在于其中。如果这些理念被个人和集体遵循，那么你肉体感官的证据将找不到矛盾。它们将把世界和存在感知为善。

quotes.text: Now: conversely, if you do not accept the idea of reincarnation, this does not mean that reincarnation is not a fact. It does mean that this insufficient energy will keep this reality at such a low ebb that it will not sufficiently materialize as an event within your system. For the egotistical self it will be a non-fact and the physical senses will of course find no Sense data to confirm reincarnation.
quotes.translation: 现在：反过来说，如果你不接受转世的理念，这并不意味着转世不是事实。这确实意味着这种不足的能量将使这个实相保持在如此低潮的状态，以至于它无法在你的系统内充分物质化为事件。对于自我本位而言，它将是一个非事实，肉体感官当然找不到任何感官资料来确认转世。

quotes.text: The main issue was the relative ease with which you were able to enlarge the hole in the glass door. Ease is the key word. To the world of the intellect, a glass door must be considered solid, as it is in the world of physical senses. In other quite as factual terms, indeed in the larger framework of facts, the door of course is not solid at all, as no objects are. Obviously that is known to science.
quotes.translation: 主要问题在于你能够相对轻松地扩大玻璃门上的洞。轻松是关键。在理智的世界里，玻璃门必须被视为固体，正如在肉体感官的世界里一样。在其他同样真实的情况下，实际上在更大的事实框架中，门当然根本不是固体，正如没有物体是固体一样。显然，科学界已知这一点。

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: Reality changes its form in order to become known to itself. It constructs varieties of consciousness with varying kinds of sense apparatus, or perceiving apparatus. Each consciousness then perceives reality in a different fashion. Yet all fashions you see are legitimate, as far as physical sense apparatus is concerned. But the mental or psychic energy behind all this is the one basic reality.
quotes.translation: 实相改变其形式以便被自身所认知。它构建了各种意识，这些意识具有不同种类的感官装置或感知装置。然后，每种意识以不同的方式感知实相。然而，就肉体感官装置而言，你所看到的所有方式都是合理的。但这一切背后的心智或心灵能量才是唯一的基本实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: lbe pure and brilliant quality of the surroundings have much in common with the appearance physical reality has for children. The physical senses seem to operate with such efficiency that you think you are awake, but their brilliancy is super perfect. As soon as you realize what type of dream this is, then with practice you can project within it consciously, and explore your environment.
quotes.translation: 环境的纯净与璀璨特质，与物质实相在孩童眼中的样貌有许多共通之处。五官感觉运作得如此高效，以至于你以为自己清醒着，但它们的辉煌是超完美的。一旦你意识到这是哪种梦境，经过练习，你就能有意识地投射其中，探索你的环境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Now: In the same way you can be allied and in tune with other probabilities that do not coincide with your space-time axis. The exterior universe with its galaxies -- as you understand it, and on that level of activity -- can be encountered on certain rigid space-time coordinates. You can visit other planets only in your present (underlined). Your present may be the past or the future as far as inhabitants of a given planet are concerned. Your physical senses will only operate in their and your present.
quotes.translation: 现在：同样地，你可以与那些不符合你时空轴的其他可能性结盟并调谐。外在宇宙及其星系——如你所理解的那样，在那个活动层面上——只能在某些固定的时空坐标上被遭遇。你只能在你的“当下”（划线强调）访问其他行星。就某个特定行星的居民而言，你的当下可能是过去或未来。你的五官感觉只会在他们和你的当下运作。

quotes.text: The traveling consciousness as you know it often experiences the feeling of motion and movement through space. This is because there is still translation through the physical senses. You are moving more effectively within camouflage reality, but you are still dealing with it. The focus of your attention has changed somewhat, but you are still strongly relating to the physical field.
quotes.translation: 你所知的旅行意识常常体验到穿越空间的运动感。这是因为仍然通过五官感觉进行转译。你在伪装实相中移动得更有效，但你仍在与之打交道。你的注意力焦点有所改变，但你仍然强烈地与物质场域相关联。

quotes.text: There are in fact infinite varieties of matter, existing in what you would call (underlined) one space framework. Using the physical senses, you can of course never perceive the existence of these other systems. Advanced training with the inner senses can lead to such explorations however. Your friend is more advanced--his system is more advanced than yours in this respect.
quotes.translation: 事实上，存在无限多种类的物质，存在于你所谓的（划线强调）一个空间框架内。使用五官感觉，你当然永远无法感知这些其他系统的存在。然而，内感官的高级训练可以引导你进行此类探索。你的朋友更先进——他的系统在这方面比你的更先进。

quotes.text: The sentence is really meaningless however, because the physical senses themselves are camouflage, you see. There would be nothing to translate. It is only use of the inner senses that will allow you to perceive under these circumstances. Theoretically, if you can bridge the gap between various reincarnations, then you can bridge the gap between your system and another.
quotes.translation: 然而，这句话其实毫无意义，因为五官感觉本身就是伪装，你明白吗。根本没有什么可转译的。只有使用内感官才能让你在这些情况下感知。理论上，如果你能跨越不同转世之间的鸿沟，那么你就能跨越你的系统与另一个系统之间的鸿沟。

quotes.text: You can, however, using your inner senses, perceive reality as it exists apart from the play and your role in it. In order to do this you must, of course, momentarily at least turn your attention away from the constant activity that is taking place -- turn off the physical senses, as it were -- and switch your attention to those events that have escaped you earlier.
quotes.translation: 然而，你可以使用你的内感官，感知实相本身——它独立于这场戏剧及你在其中的角色而存在。要做到这一点，你当然必须至少暂时将注意力从正在发生的持续活动中移开——可以说是关闭五官感觉——并将注意力转向那些你先前错过的那些事件。

quotes.text: I have told you that many other systems coexist with your own, simultaneously. Other fields exist that you do not perceive with your physical senses. Ruburt was not perceiving with his own physical senses, nor was he asleep.

quotes.text: What you perceive with your physical senses is of course real. It is one of the forms that reality takes. But if you insist upon believing that only physical data is real, then you see you will never learn what is within it.
quotes.translation: 你用肉体感官感知到的当然是真实的。那是实相所采取的形式之一。但如果你坚持只相信物质资料才是真实的，那么你看，你将永远无法了解其中蕴含着什么。

quotes.text: Very well. Your idea of time is false. Time as you experience it is an illusion caused by your own physical senses. Your physical senses force you to perceive action in certain terms, but this is not the nature of action.

quotes.text: This does not mean that it cannot be seen on occasion, but it cannot be perceived through use of the unaided physical senses. It is that part which in physical life is intertwined with the physical image.

It is the learning process that conditions you to translate a given stimuli into data that will be picked up by a given physical sense; that is, translations always occur in any case.
quotes.translation: 这同样适用于看，显然也适用于感觉或触摸。不言而喻，这种潜能极少被实现，但它是人类遗产的一部分。正是学习过程使你习惯于将特定刺激转化为能被特定肉体感官接收的资料；也就是说，在任何情况下，转化始终都在发生。

quotes.translation: 现在。在相当长一段时间内，不太可能有人像菲利普听到我说话那样听到我的声音。再说一次，当时的情况恰好很有利。我告诉过你，所有行动都是心灵行动，但所用术语并无多大区别。所有行动确实具有电磁实相，而这通常不被肉体感官所感知。

quotes.text: It is learning the methods of physical perception, as within you it experiences your reactions and becomes familiar with the ways in which you use physical senses to obtain information. quotes.translation: 它正在学习物质感知的方法，因为在你之内它体验着你的反应，并逐渐熟悉你运用物质感官获取信息的方式。它正在以或多或少忠实于所需样貌的复制品来塑造自己的身体。（停顿）它正以你自己的许多期望为模型，也以它父亲的许多期望为模型。

quotes.text: Now: On certain occasions, sometimes near the point of death, but often simply in conscious states outside of the body, man is able to perceive that kind of light. In some out-of-body experiences Ruburt, for example, saw colors more dazzling than any physical ones, and you saw the same kind of colors in your dream. They are a part of your inner senses' larger spectrum of perception, and in the dream state you were not relying upon your physical senses at all.
quotes.translation: 现在：在某些时刻，有时接近死亡之际，但往往只是处于身体外的意识状态时，人能够感知到那种光。例如，在一些出体体验中，鲁柏曾看到比任何物质色彩更耀眼的颜色，而你在梦中也曾看到同样的颜色。它们是你内在感官更广阔感知光谱的一部分，而在梦境状态中，你完全未依赖物质感官。

quotes.text: Our psychological structures are different, practically speaking, in that we consciously utilize a multidimensional psychological reality that you inherently possess, but are unfamiliar with at an egotistical level. It is natural, then, that our environment would have multidimensional qualities that the physical senses would never perceive.
quotes.translation: 实际上，我们的心理结构是不同的，因为我们有意识地运用着一种多维度的心理实相——你本就拥有这种实相，但在自我层面却对它不熟悉。因此，我们的环境自然具有物质感官永远无法感知的多维特质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Time, as you know it, is a distortion brought about by the operation of the physical senses. Direct experience has no need of physical time. You exist now, period. The past, the present and the future as you know them are illusion, and yet they exist now. You can influence the past today; you can have memory of the future.
quotes.translation: 你所认知的时间，是物质感官运作所导致的扭曲。直接体验不需要物质时间。你存在于当下，仅此而已。你所知的过去、现在与未来皆是幻相，然而它们都存在于当下。你今天可以影响过去；你可以拥有对未来的记忆。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Now without your particular physical senses you would not perceive the couch as solid. Consciousness that has different perceptive mechanisms than your own is unaware of our now famous blue couch. You make the organization. Your thoughts perceive an organization. You enforce the organization, and indeed create it.
quotes.translation: 现在，若没有你特定的物质感官，你就不会将沙发感知为固体。拥有与你不同感知机制的意识，根本不知道我们那著名的蓝色沙发。你组织了这一切。你的思想感知到一种组织。你强化了这种组织，并确实创造了它。

quotes.text: The other, older perceptive mechanisms still linger on however. The ego does not and cannot make use of them. Indeed the ego is almost entirely formed by the physical senses method of perception. Other layers of the self biologically respond to the older, more generalized but less limited perceptive mechanisms.
quotes.translation: 然而，其他更古老的感知机制依然残留着。自我并不使用也无法使用它们。事实上，自我几乎完全由物质感官的感知方式所形成。自我的其他层面在生物学上会回应那些更古老、更普遍但限制较少的感知机制。

quotes.text: In psychological time therefore it is at least possible that you can have some experience with other methods when you close off habitual methods of perception. quotes.translation: 因此在心理时间里，当你关闭惯常的感知方式时，至少有可能体验到其他方法。然而，自我紧紧依附于物质感官的使用。在梦境状态中，其他方法也至少能被轻微体验到。

quotes.text: It is therefore theoretically possible for you to see the world through a frog's eye, or a bird's or an ant's. We are speaking here of physical senses. The inner self has also latent inner senses beside the ones that it normally uses while the consciousness is tuned into a particular camouflage system.
quotes.translation: 因此理论上，你可以通过青蛙的眼睛、鸟的眼睛或蚂蚁的眼睛来看世界。我们这里说的是物质感官。当意识调谐到特定的伪装系统时，内在自体除了通常使用的感官外，还拥有潜在的内在感官。

quotes.text: As a rule there is little communication, you see, through those uncamouflaged or undifferentiated areas. They act in fact as boundaries, even while they represent the basic stuff of which all camouflage is composed. Without the camouflage, you see, you would perceive nothing using the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 通常来说，通过这些未经伪装或未分化的区域几乎没有交流。它们实际上充当了边界，即使它们代表了所有伪装构成的基本材料。没有伪装，你根本无法使用物质感官感知任何事物。

quotes.text: What often happens is that your conception of reality is so limited that you take fright whenever you perceive any experience that does not fit into your conception. Now I am not speaking merely of abilities loosely called "extrasensory perception." These experiences seem extraordinary to you only because you have for so long denied the existence of any perception that did not come through the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 经常发生的情况是，你对现实的观念如此局限，以至于每当感知到任何不符合你观念的经验时，你就会惊慌失措。现在我说的不仅仅是那些被笼统称为“超感官知觉”的能力。这些经验对你来说显得非凡，只是因为你长久以来否定了任何不通过物质感官而来的感知的存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner senses can and do perceive this reality in an undistorted fashion. Your brain is itself a camouflage pattern. It can only translate and perceive what seems to be the evidence of the physical senses. It cannot step outside of itself. （Smile.） It is that which it attempts to investigate.
quotes.translation: 内在感官能够并且确实以未扭曲的方式感知这个实相。你的大脑本身就是一个伪装模式。它只能翻译和感知看似来自物质感官的证据。它无法超越自身。（微笑。）它正是它试图调查的对象。

quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: These are the ground rules, so to speak. Your physical mechanisms are equipped to function in such a way that reality is perceived through the lens of particular root assumptions. Using the physical senses alone, it is almost literally impossible for you to perceive reality in any other manner.
quotes.translation: 这些可以说是基本规则。你的物质机制被设计成以这样的方式运作：实相通过特定根本假设的透镜被感知。仅使用物质感官，你几乎不可能以任何其他方式感知实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: For you look now at Ruburt. There are indeed, now, in this room there are five physical Ruburts. For each of you, including Ruburt, create and project your own image of him, and each of you perceive with your physical senses only that image of him which you have individually created.
quotes.translation: 因为你现在看着鲁柏。确实，此刻在这房间里存在着五个物质性的鲁柏。因为你们每个人，包括鲁柏在内，都创造并投射出你们自己对他的形象，而你们每个人都仅以肉体感官感知你们各自创造的那个形象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Some such data will never be utilized. Some such data will be utilized time and time again. Now you see the energy does not reside within any given physical object, but in the idea form that is within it. Physical objects do not exist as such in a basic manner. You can only perceive large areas of reality data however by transforming it into terms that can be picked up by the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 有些这类资料永远不会被利用。有些这类资料会一次又一次地被利用。现在你明白，能量并不驻留在任何特定物质对象中，而是存在于其内在的意念形态里。物质对象在根本意义上并不以这样的形式存在。然而，你只能通过将大范围的实相资料转化为能被肉体感官接收的术语来感知它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You were dealing, indeed, with a legitimate perception into a dimension outside of the ones with which you are familiar, and with a distortion of a sort on your part, as once again you attempted to translate inner data into a form that could be perceived by the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 你确实在处理一个对熟悉维度之外维度的合法感知，同时你这边也存在着某种扭曲，因为你再次试图将内在资料转化为能被肉体感官感知的形式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Frederick however could also be reacting to the same sort of event that has not yet occurred, however, to his perception--that is, to an event in the future. For the mind does not divide time into a series of moments. This is done by the physical brain and the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 然而弗雷德里克也可能在反应同一类尚未发生的事件——即对他感知而言的未来事件。因为心灵并不将时间分割成一系列时刻。这是由物质大脑和肉体感官完成的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: I have told you that exterior reality is a materialization of inner electromagnetic reality. I have told you that all experience is electromagnetically coded. The inner senses pick up this data directly, you see, as in last Friday's episode. Some translation must occur if you are to become aware consciously of such material, or if it is to impress the physical senses at all.
quotes.translation: 我曾告诉你们外在实相是内在电磁实相的物质化。我曾告诉你们所有经验都以电磁方式编码。内在感官直接接收这些资料，你看，就像上周五的插曲那样。若要你有意识地觉察这类材料，或让它对肉体感官产生印象，就必须发生某种翻译。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: As a rule they are not solid in the same way that clouds are not solid, and yet they have shape, and to a certain extent boundaries, and of course movement. They definitely have a reality, you see, though you cannot usually perceive it with the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 通常它们并不坚实，就像云朵并不坚实一样，然而它们有形状，在某种程度上也有边界，当然还有运动。它们确实具有实相，你看，尽管你通常无法用肉体感官感知到它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You seem to perceive exclusively through your physical senses, and yet you have only to extend your egotistical idea of reality, and you will find even your egotistical self accepting quite readily the existence of nonphysical information.
quotes.translation: 你似乎只透过肉体感官来感知，然而你只需扩展你对实相的自大观念，就会发现连你那个自大的自我也很容易接受非物质信息的存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you dwell upon limitations, then you will meet them. You must create a new picture in your mind. It will differ from the picture your physical senses may show you at any given time, precisely in those areas where changes are required.
quotes.translation: 如果你执着于限制，你就会遭遇它们。你必须在心中创造一幅新的画面。这幅画面与你肉体感官在任何特定时刻可能展现给你的画面，恰恰在需要改变的那些领域有所不同。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your physical senses, again, act almost like a biological alphabet, allowing you to organize and perceive certain kinds of information from which you form the events of your world and the contours of your reality.
quotes.translation: 你的肉体感官再次像一套生物字母表，让你能够组织并感知某些信息，并从中构建你世界的事件和你实相的轮廓。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In that literally power-packed few hours, he also knew that the physical senses did not so much perceive concrete phenomena, but actually had a hand in the creation of events that were then perceived as actual.
quotes.translation: 在那充满能量的几个小时里，他也知道肉体感官与其说是在感知具体的现象，不如说是在参与创造那些随后被感知为真实的事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The physical senses have to screen out such perceptions. That light, however, is literally everywhere at once, and it is a "knowing light," as Ruburt's [William] James perceived.3
quotes.translation: 肉体感官必须过滤掉这类感知。然而，那种光确实同时存在于各处，它是一种“知晓之光”，正如鲁柏的[威廉]詹姆斯所感知到的那样。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
